review.citation: Said K, et al. Non-vitamin K antagonist oral anticoagulants after transcatheter aortic valve replacement: a network meta-analysis. Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews. 2024.

identity.title: Non-vitamin K antagonist oral anticoagulants after transcatheter aortic valve replacement

question.naturalLanguageInput: What are the benefits and harms of non-vitamin K oral anticoagulants after transcatheter aortic valve replacement?
question.structuredInput: In adults after successful TAVR, with or without another indication for anticoagulation, compare a non-vitamin K oral anticoagulant with antiplatelet therapy or a vitamin K antagonist for mortality, stroke, thromboembolism, and major bleeding; include randomized controlled trials.
eligibilityCriteria: Adults after transcatheter aortic valve replacement
Antiplatelet therapy or vitamin K antagonist comparator
Reports mortality, stroke, thromboembolism, or major bleeding

population: adults after transcatheter aortic valve replacement
